agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Proposal to change delivery ETA display from exact timestamps (e.g., 'Delivery by 14:30 on Friday') to date-only windows (e.g., 'Delivery by Friday'). This is a fundamental change to the delivery promise made to customers — the core contract between a courier/logistics company and its end users.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking & Customer Communication — the bounded context responsible for surfacing delivery commitments to customers, partners, and internal operations.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"ETA","definition":"Estimated Time of Arrival — the predicted point in time when a delivery will reach the recipient. In courier/logistics, this is a core promise that drives customer behavior and operational planning."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"A time range (or date range) within which a delivery is expected. Can be precise (2-hour window) or broad (full day). The granularity directly impacts customer trust and operational flexibility."},{"term":"Delivery Promise","definition":"The commitment made to the customer about when their package will arrive. This is the single most visible metric in last-mile logistics and the primary driver of customer satisfaction."},{"term":"SLA","definition":"Service Level Agreement — contractual commitments to partners or enterprise customers about delivery timing. Often specifies exact time windows, not just dates."},{"term":"Last-Mile Delivery","definition":"The final leg of the delivery journey from distribution hub to the end recipient. The most expensive, most variable, and most customer-visible segment."},{"term":"Proof of Delivery","definition":"Confirmation that a delivery was completed, often timestamped. Used for SLA compliance verification."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A delivery promise, once communicated to a customer, defines the service contract for that delivery. Degrading the precision of that promise is a material change to the service offering.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy about promise granularity, not a data integrity constraint. A VP could decide to change precision levels.","testable_assertion":"When a delivery ETA is displayed, it must match the currently configured precision level (timestamp or date-only) consistently across all customer-facing channels."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Enterprise/B2B customers with SLA contracts that specify time-window delivery guarantees must continue to receive time-precise ETAs regardless of any default display change.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Contractual SLA obligations are legally binding. Violating them causes financial penalties and contract breaches — this is a hard constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery linked to an enterprise SLA with time-window terms, when the ETA is displayed, then it MUST include a time component, not just a date."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Any ETA displayed must be achievable by operations. Showing a date-only window when internal routing still plans to a specific time creates a disconnect between what's promised and what's tracked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an operational alignment policy, not a system integrity rule.","testable_assertion":"When a date-only ETA is shown externally, internal operational targets may still use timestamps, but the external promise must be a superset (less precise) of the internal target — never more precise."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Delivery notifications (dispatched, out-for-delivery, delivered) must use consistent precision with the original ETA promise. If the promise was date-only, notifications should not suddenly introduce exact times.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a UX consistency policy, changeable by product decision.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery with a date-only ETA, when any notification is sent for that delivery, then no specific delivery time is mentioned in the notification body."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium/express service tiers that charge more for time-definite delivery (e.g., 'by 10:30 AM') must retain exact-time ETAs as part of their value proposition.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Customers pay a premium specifically for time-definite service. Removing the time component while charging the premium price would be deceptive and likely violates consumer protection rules.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery using a premium/express service tier with time-definite guarantees, when the ETA is displayed, then it MUST include a time component."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A standard (non-premium, non-SLA) delivery","when":"the customer views the delivery ETA","then":"the ETA is displayed as a date-only window (e.g., 'Delivery by Friday') with no time component"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An enterprise delivery governed by an SLA with time-window terms","when":"the customer or partner views the delivery ETA","then":"the ETA is displayed with full timestamp precision per the SLA contract"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A premium/express service tier delivery with time-definite guarantee","when":"the customer views the delivery ETA","then":"the ETA includes the guaranteed time (e.g., 'Delivery by 10:30 AM on Friday')"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A standard delivery with a date-only ETA","when":"any customer-facing notification (email, SMS, push) is sent","then":"the notification uses date-only language consistent with the original promise"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"The ETA display change is deployed","when":"historical deliveries are viewed in order history","then":"past deliveries retain their original ETA format (timestamp) and are not retroactively reformatted"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A delivery is in progress spanning a timezone boundary","when":"the date-only ETA is displayed","then":"the date shown is calculated in the recipient's local timezone, not UTC or sender timezone"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Same-day delivery — showing 'Delivery by Today' without a time is far less useful than 'Delivery by 14:30 today'","expected_behavior":"Same-day deliveries should retain time-level precision since the entire value proposition is speed and the date-only window is meaningless (customer already knows it's today)."},{"scenario":"Delivery spanning midnight — a package due at 01:00 Saturday could display as 'by Saturday' making it seem like anytime Saturday, hiding that it's imminent","expected_behavior":"When delivery is imminent (within a few hours), switch to a more precise display regardless of default setting. The ETA should reflect urgency."},{"scenario":"Multi-package orders with different ETAs — one arrives Thursday, another Friday. Date-only may cause confusion vs timestamps that clearly sequence arrivals","expected_behavior":"Multi-package orders should clearly indicate which package arrives on which date, and if both arrive the same date, consider retaining time precision to differentiate."},{"scenario":"Recipient availability coordination — customers who plan to be home at a specific time lose the ability to plan around exact timestamps","expected_behavior":"Consider offering a 'view detailed estimate' option that reveals a time range for customers who need to plan availability."},{"scenario":"Failed delivery rescheduled — after a failed attempt, the new ETA is date-only but customer needs to know when to be available","expected_behavior":"Rescheduled deliveries should offer time-window selection or at minimum a time range (AM/PM), not just a date."},{"scenario":"International deliveries crossing date lines — 'by Friday' is ambiguous when sender and recipient are in different hemispheres","expected_behavior":"Always calculate and display the date in the recipient's local timezone with clear timezone indication for international shipments."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"NEGATIVE RISK: Premium service tiers lose differentiation if all tiers show the same date-only format — potential revenue erosion on premium upsells. Reduced precision may decrease 'delivery by X' guarantee claims but also reduces perceived value. POSITIVE: Fewer missed-ETA refund claims since date-only windows are easier to hit, potentially saving 5-15% of current refund/credit costs tied to missed time-specific promises.","operational":"POSITIVE: Significant operational relief. Exact-time ETAs create rigid routing constraints and force costly last-mile adjustments. Date-only windows give dispatchers and drivers more flexibility to optimize routes, batch deliveries, and absorb delays without triggering 'late' status. NEGATIVE: Internal systems still need timestamp-level planning — this change only affects the external promise, so operational complexity doesn't fully disappear. Risk of operational complacency if teams treat date-only as permission to deprioritize.","user":"NEGATIVE: Major regression in customer experience for time-sensitive recipients. Industry trend is toward MORE precision (Amazon, UPS, FedEx all offer time windows or live tracking). Moving to less precision goes against market direction. Customers who coordinate their day around deliveries (work-from-home, medical supplies, business-critical) lose critical planning ability. POSITIVE: Reduced anxiety from countdown-style exact ETAs that frequently shift by minutes, causing notification fatigue.","risk":"HIGH: Competitive disadvantage — competitors offering 2-hour windows will appear superior. MEDIUM: Enterprise SLA compliance risk if the change accidentally applies to contractual deliveries. MEDIUM: Customer churn among power users and frequent recipients who rely on time precision. LOW-MEDIUM: Regulatory risk in jurisdictions that require specific delivery time commitments for certain goods (perishables, pharmaceuticals).","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End consumers (recipients)","impact":"Lose ability to plan their day around deliveries. Particularly harmful for recipients of time-sensitive goods, those in shared buildings requiring presence, and anyone coordinating multiple deliveries."},{"group":"Enterprise/B2B partners","impact":"Must be explicitly carved out from this change. Any accidental degradation of SLA reporting precision could trigger contract penalties and partner churn."},{"group":"Customer support team","impact":"Expect increased 'when exactly will my package arrive?' inquiries. Date-only ETAs shift precision demand from the UI to the support channel, increasing call/chat volume."},{"group":"Drivers/couriers","impact":"Positive — more flexible delivery sequencing. Less pressure from customer-visible exact-time commitments. Better route optimization potential."},{"group":"Operations/dispatch","impact":"Mixed — external promise is relaxed but internal planning still needs timestamp precision. Must maintain two tiers of ETA data."},{"group":"Product/marketing","impact":"Must reposition messaging. Premium tier differentiation becomes more important. Risk of negative press if perceived as service degradation."},{"group":"Finance/claims team","impact":"Likely reduction in time-based SLA miss claims and associated refunds for standard tier. Need new reporting to track date-level accuracy vs former time-level accuracy."}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The proposal is underspecified. It does not clarify whether this applies universally or only to standard tier, does not address same-day delivery, does not mention SLA/enterprise carve-outs, and does not state whether this is a UI-only change or also affects API responses and partner integrations. The core business intent is clear but the scope and exceptions are ambiguous, warranting escalation.","high_confidence_areas":["Enterprise SLA deliveries must retain time precision — this is contractually non-negotiable","Premium time-definite services must retain time ETAs — the customer pays for this","Operational flexibility will improve for standard deliveries","Customer support volume will increase"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether same-day deliveries are included in this change","Whether API consumers and partner integrations are affected or only end-user UI","Whether this applies to all geographies or specific markets","Whether there is a fallback mechanism for customers who need time-level precision","What the rollout strategy is — big bang vs gradual"],"assumptions":["This change is intended for standard/economy service tiers only","Enterprise SLA contracts with time-window terms exist and must be honored","The company offers premium service tiers with time-definite guarantees","Internal operational systems will continue to track timestamp-level ETAs","The change affects customer-facing displays, not internal operational tools"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The proposal does not specify which service tiers are affected. Applying date-only ETAs to premium/express tiers would destroy their value proposition and potentially violate consumer protection rules. Same-day delivery with date-only ETAs is essentially meaningless.","affected_stakeholders":["End consumers","Enterprise partners","Product team","Finance"],"options":["A) Apply only to standard/economy tier, retain time ETAs for premium and same-day","B) Apply universally to all tiers","C) Apply to standard tier and offer optional time-window detail on demand"],"recommendation":"Option A — standard tier only with explicit carve-outs for premium, same-day, and SLA-bound deliveries. Option C as a Phase 2 enhancement."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No information on whether this change affects API responses consumed by partners and integrations, or only the end-user UI. Partner integrations may depend on timestamp-precision ETAs for their own systems.","affected_stakeholders":["Enterprise partners","Integration consumers","Engineering team"],"options":["A) UI-only change, API continues returning timestamps","B) API also changes to date-only","C) API returns both, with a new field for date-only display"],"recommendation":"Option A for safety — change UI display only, keep API contracts unchanged. Add an optional date-only field to API if partners request it."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No rollout or rollback strategy specified. A change this visible to customers needs A/B testing or gradual rollout to measure impact on NPS, support volume, and conversion before full deployment.","affected_stakeholders":["End consumers","Customer support","Product team"],"options":["A) Big bang rollout","B) A/B test with 10% of traffic first","C) Gradual rollout by market/region"],"recommendation":"Option B — A/B test measuring support contact rate, NPS delta, and delivery satisfaction scores before full rollout."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Proposal to change delivery ETA display from exact timestamps (e.g., 'Delivery by 14:30 on Friday') to date-only windows (e.g., 'Delivery by Friday'). This is a fundamental change to the delivery promise made to customers — the core contract between a courier/logistics company and its end users.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking & Customer Communication — the bounded context responsible for surfacing delivery commitments to customers, partners, and int
